    69→
    70→```bash
    71→cargo build
    72→cargo test
    73→cargo clippy -- -D warnings
    74→```
    75→
    76→Edition 2024. Never suppress warnings.
    77→
    78→## Key Reference
    79→
    80→Read `docs/research.txt` for the full design research and saga breakdown.
    81→Read `docs/papers.txt` for the source paper link.
    82→

1→# tc24r - C compiler for COR24 (24-bit RISC) ISA
     2→
     3→## Build & Test
     4→
     5→- No workspace-level Cargo.toml; build individual components:
     6→  `cargo build --manifest-path components/cli/Cargo.toml --release`
     7→- Run reg-rs regression tests: `reg-rs run -p tc24r`
     8→- Run a single demo: `bash demos/run-demo.sh`
     9→
    10→## Key Rules
    11→
    12→- **Never use `sed` in test scripts or demo scripts.** Use `awk`, `grep`, and `tr` instead. BSD sed (macOS) and GNU sed (Linux) have incompatible extensions (`\s`, `-E` vs `-r`, etc.) that break cross-platform testing. All demo scripts have been ported to use `awk` for field extraction.
    13→- reg-rs baselines (`.out` files) contain absolute paths. After switching machines, run `reg-rs rebase -p tc24r` to update baselines.
    14→
    15→## Project Structure
    16→
    17→- `components/` - Rust crates organized by compiler phase (core, frontend, backend, codegen-*, cli, testing)
    18→- `demos/` - 46 end-to-end demo scripts (run-demo*.sh) with C source files
    19→- `work/reg-rs/` - reg-rs regression test definitions (.rgt) and baselines (.out)
    20→- `docs/` - Design docs, testing strategy, known issues
    21→

    41→
    42→## Project: tf24a — Tiny Forth for COR24 in Assembler
    43→
    44→Clean-room DTC Forth for the COR24 24-bit RISC ISA. Assembler kernel, self-extending in Forth.
    45→
    46→### Tools
    47→- `cor24-run --run file.s [opts]` — assemble and run COR24 assembly
    48→- `cor24-run --run file.s --dump --speed 0 -n N` — run N instructions, dump state
    49→- `cor24-run --run file.s --terminal --echo --speed 0` — interactive UART session
    50→- `cor24-run --run file.s -u 'input' --speed 0 --dump` — feed UART input, dump state
    51→
    52→### COR24 Assembly Syntax
    53→- Labels on own line: `label:` (no inline `label: instr`)
    54→- Comments: `;` only (not `#`)
    55→- Decimal immediates (not hex): `la r0, -65280` not `la r0, 0xFF0100`
    56→- `.word label` — emits 24-bit address (one label per directive)
    57→- `.byte 72, 101, 108` — raw bytes (no string literals)
    58→- No `.align` directive; manually pad with `.byte 0`
    59→
    60→### Register Allocation (Frozen)
    61→- r0 = W (work register) / scratch
    62→- r1 = RSP (return stack pointer, SRAM ~0x0F0000 growing down)
    63→- r2 = IP (instruction pointer for threaded code)
    64→- sp = DSP (data stack, hardware push/pop in EBR)
    65→- fp = available as extra scratch
    66→
    67→### COR24 ISA — Register Capabilities (MUST follow)
    68→
    69→**Load destinations:** only r0, r1, r2 (NOT fp, sp)
    70→- `lc r0, imm8` / `lcu r0, imm8` / `la r0, imm24` — ✓ for r0, r1, r2
    71→- `lw r0, off(base)` / `lb`, `lbu` — destination must be r0, r1, or r2
    72→- `lc fp, ...` / `lw fp, ...` — **ILLEGAL**, will not assemble
    73→
    74→**ALU destinations:** only r0, r1, r2
    75→- `add r0, r2` / `sub r0, r2` / `and`, `or`, `xor` — ✓ for r0, r1, r2
    76→- `add fp, ...` / `sub fp, ...` — **ILLEGAL**
    77→- `add r0, imm8` — works for r0, r1, r2, sp (NOT fp)
    78→
    79→**Comparisons:** `ceq ra, rb`, `clu ra, rb`, `cls ra, rb`
    80→- ra and rb can be r0, r1, r2, fp, sp, z
    81→- Use `ceq r0, z` to test zero
    82→
    83→**Stack:** `push ra` / `pop ra` — ra can be r0, r1, r2, fp
    84→- `push fp` and `pop fp` work (this is how to move fp ↔ r0)
    85→
    86→**fp as base register:** `lw r0, off(fp)` / `sw r0, off(fp)` — ✓
    87→- fp is the ONLY way to index into EBR stack memory
    88→
    89→**Reading sp:** `mov fp, sp` copies sp to fp. Then `push fp; pop r0` gets the value into r0.
    90→- There is NO `mov r0, sp` instruction
    91→- `mov sp, fp` restores sp from fp
    92→
    93→**Key constraints:**
    94→- Branch offset ±127 bytes (signed 8-bit); use `la r0, label; jmp (r0)` for far jumps
    95→- `jal r1,(r0)` conflicts with r1=RSP — do not use for subroutine calls
    96→- Cell size = 3 bytes (24-bit words)
    97→- sp inits at 0xFEEC00, grows down
    98→
    99→See `docs/inspect-stack-impl.md` for full ISA reference.
   100→
   101→### Development Rules — TDD Required
   102→
   103→**Every new word or feature MUST have a test before implementation.**
   104→
   105→1. Write the test first as a threaded-code sequence in the test_thread, or as a `cor24-run -u` command
   106→2. Verify the test fails or produces wrong output
   107→3. Implement the word
   108→4. Verify the test passes
   109→5. Run ALL previous tests to check for regressions
   110→
   111→Test format for `cor24-run -u`:
   112→```bash
   113→# Test: WORD ( inputs -- expected-outputs )
   114→cor24-run --run forth.s -u 'inputs\n' --speed 0 -n 5000000 2>&1 | grep "^UART output:"
   115→# Expected: ... expected output ...
   116→```
   117→
   118→When adding threaded-code tests, add them to test_thread BEFORE the `do_quit` entry.
   119→
   120→**Run the full test suite with:** `reg-rs run -p tf24a --parallel`
   121→
   122→Regression tests use `reg-rs` (golden-output regression tool). Each test captures
   123→the full cor24-run output and compares against baseline. Tests are in `~/.local/reg-rs/`.
   124→
   125→To add a new test:
   126→```bash
   127→PP="grep -A 100 '^UART output:' || true"
   128→reg-rs create -t tf24a_TESTNAME -P "$PP" --timeout 30 \
   129→  -c "cor24-run --run forth.s -u 'INPUT\n' --speed 0 -n 5000000 2>&1" \
   130→  --desc "description"
   131→```
   132→
   133→Legacy bash tests: `./demo.sh test`
   134→
   135→**Stack leak tests are mandatory.** Every new word must be tested for stack balance:
   136→```bash
   137→# Before and after calling NEWWORD, DEPTH must not change
   138→cor24-run --run forth.s -u 'DEPTH .\nNEWWORD\nDEPTH .\n' --speed 0 -n 10000000
   139→```
   140→
   141→**Common COR24 stack bugs:**
   142→- Using `push r2` (DS) instead of `sw r2, 0(r1); add r1, -3` (RS) to save IP
   143→- WORD's eol_flag path must pop exactly one RS entry (saved IP), not more
   144→- Any `push`/`pop` inside a primitive changes sp — account for this in DEPTH/.S
   145→
   146→### DTC Inner Interpreter
   147→```
   148→; NEXT (inline everywhere or as tail of each primitive):
   149→;   lw r0, 0(r2)    ; W = mem[IP] — fetch CFA from thread
   150→;   add r2, 3       ; IP += cell
   151→;   jmp (r0)        ; execute code at CFA
   152→```
   153→
   154→### UART I/O
   155→- Data register: address -65280 (0xFF0100)
   156→- Status register: address -65279 (0xFF0101)
   157→- TX busy: bit 7 of status
   158→- RX ready: bit 0 of status
   159→

    15→
    16→Pre-commit gate (all must pass): `cargo test && cargo clippy -- -D warnings && cargo fmt --check`
    17→
    18→## Two-Layer Architecture
    19→
    20→See `docs/architecture.md` for full details.
    21→
    22→**Layer 1 (this repo)**: Generic inference-time learning engine. Task-agnostic
    23→orchestration, dual-memory (skills + experiences), ICRL trajectory injection,
    24→distillation. Does NOT know about specific domains (TTS, ffmpeg, etc.).
    25→
    26→**Layer 2 (separate repos)**: Domain-specific knowledge. Skill documents,
    27→curated experiences, executor implementations, validators. See
    28→`docs/domain-repos.md` for the domain repo contract.
    29→
    30→## Crate Layout
    31→
    32→Cargo workspace (`edition = "2024"`) with five crates under `crates/`:
    33→
    34→- **agentrail-core** -- Domain types and error enum. All other crates depend on this. Key types: `SagaConfig`, `StepConfig`, `StepRole`, `Trajectory`, `HandoffPacket`, `JobSpec`. Error type: `agentrail_core::error::Error` with `Result<T>` alias.
    35→- **agentrail-store** -- File-based persistence against `.agentrail/`. Modules: `saga` (init/load/save), `step` (create/transition/list with NNN-slug dirs), `trajectory` (ICRL record save/retrieve), `session` (Claude Code JSONL snapshot).
    36→- **agentrail-cli** -- Binary crate (`agentrail`). 8 commands: init, status, next, begin, complete, plan, history, abort. Has `lib.rs` exporting `commands` module for testability.
    37→- **agentrail-exec** -- Deterministic step executors (stub; will become trait + shell executor routing to domain repos).
    38→- **agentrail-validate** -- Output validators (stub; will become trait + shell validator routing to domain repos).
    39→
    40→Dependency flow: `cli -> store, exec, validate -> core`
    41→
    42→## Key Concepts
    43→
    44→- **Dual memory (XSkill pattern)**: skills (strategic workflow docs per task category) + experiences (tactical per-run records). See `docs/dual-memory.md`.
    45→- **Step roles** (Meta, Production, Deterministic, Validation): orchestration loop. Meta prepares handoffs, production does semantic work, deterministic runs without agents, validation checks outputs.
    46→- **ICRL injection**: `agentrail next` retrieves successful experiences for the step's task_type and injects them into the prompt output.
    47→- **Step transitions** enforce: Pending -> InProgress -> Completed|Blocked.
    48→- **Domain repos**: per-domain knowledge (skills, experiences, executors, validators) in separate repos. See `docs/domain-repos.md`.
    49→
    50→## Storage Layout
    51→
    52→All runtime data in `.agentrail/` (never `.avoid-compaction/`):
    53→```
    54→.agentrail/saga.toml
    55→.agentrail/plan.md
    56→.agentrail/steps/NNN-slug/{step.toml, prompt.md, summary.md}
    57→.agentrail/skills/{task_type}.toml          (planned)
    58→.agentrail/experiences/{task_type}/run_NNN.json  (planned)
    59→.agentrail/trajectories/{task_type}/run_NNN.json (existing, backward compat)
    60→.agentrail/domains.toml                     (planned)
    61→.agentrail/sessions/{session-id}.jsonl
    62→```
    63→
    64→## Key Documentation
    65→
    66→- `docs/architecture.md` -- Two-layer design, layer interaction, domain discovery
    67→- `docs/dual-memory.md` -- Skills and experiences schemas, retrieval strategy, distillation
    68→- `docs/domain-repos.md` -- Domain repo structure, executor/validator interfaces
    69→- `docs/research-foundations.md` -- Research papers and how they map to the architecture
    70→- `docs/implementation-plan.md` -- Phased roadmap (replaces continue-from-bootstrap.md phases)
    71→- `docs/continue-from-bootstrap.md` -- Original bootstrapping handoff (historical)
    72→
    73→## Development Practices
    74→
    75→- TDD: write failing test first, implement minimum logic, refactor.
    76→- Test pattern: integration tests using `tempfile::tempdir()`.
    77→- Evolved from [avoid-compaction](https://github.com/softwarewrighter/avoid-compaction).
    78→- Layer 1 must remain task-agnostic. No domain-specific imports in agentrail-rs.
    79→

1→Build a browser-based UI for Tiny Macro Lisp on COR24. Phase 1: compile tml24c to WASM via Emscripten or rewrite core in Rust. Phase 2: REPL component. Phase 3: visual execution trace.

1→Build a p-code VM and p-code assembler (pasm) for the COR24 24-bit RISC ISA. The VM is a stack-based interpreter written in COR24 assembly. The pasm tool (also COR24 assembly) assembles human-readable .pasm source into bytecode for testing the VM. Pascal compiler is a separate project.

1→# tf24a: Tiny Forth for COR24 in Assembler
     2→
     3→DTC Forth targeting COR24-TB via cor24-run assembler/emulator.
     4→Clean-room implementation, assembler kernel, self-extending in Forth.
     5→
     6→## Design Decisions (Frozen)
     7→- DTC (Direct Threaded Code)
     8→- No TOS cache (only 3 GP registers)
     9→- sp = data stack (hardware push/pop in EBR)
    10→- r1 = return stack pointer (SRAM region)
    11→- r2 = IP (instruction pointer)
    12→- r0 = W / scratch
    13→- 24-bit cell (3 bytes)
    14→- Little-endian
    15→- Entry point at address 0
    16→
    17→## Memory Layout
    18→- 0x000000+: bootstrap code, primitives, dictionary (grows up via HERE)
    19→- ~0x0F0000: return stack (grows down via r1)
    20→- EBR (sp): data stack (hardware push/pop)
    21→- 0xFF0100: UART data, 0xFF0101: UART status
    22→
    23→## Phases
    24→
    25→### Phase 1: Bootstrap & Stacks
    26→- Entry point, register init, UART I/O (KEY, EMIT)
    27→- Data stack (push/pop) and return stack (r1-based) primitives
    28→- NEXT macro, halt pattern
    29→- Test: echo characters, stack push/pop verified via --dump
    30→
    31→### Phase 2: Inner Interpreter & Primitives
    32→- NEXT, DOCOL (inline per colon word), EXIT
    33→- LIT, BRANCH, 0BRANCH
    34→- Arithmetic: + - AND OR XOR = < 0=
    35→- Stack ops: DROP DUP SWAP OVER >R R> R@
    36→- Memory: @ ! C@ C!
    37→- Test: hand-assembled colon definitions execute correctly
    38→
    39→### Phase 3: Dictionary & Compiler
    40→- Dictionary header layout (link, flags+len, name, code field)
    41→- HERE, LATEST, STATE, BASE variables
    42→- FIND (dictionary search)
    43→- Number parser
    44→- : and ; (colon compiler)
    45→- , (comma - compile cell) and ALLOT
    46→- CREATE, IMMEDIATE, [ ]
    47→- Test: define and run : SQUARE DUP * ;
    48→
    49→### Phase 4: Text Interpreter (QUIT)
    50→- Token parser (WORD equivalent)
    51→- Interpret/compile dispatch
    52→- QUIT outer loop
    53→- Error handling (? for unknown words)
    54→- Test: interactive session via --terminal: 1 2 + . prints 3
    55→
    56→### Phase 5: Self-Hosted Growth (Forth source)
    57→- . (dot - print number), CR, SPACE, SPACES
    58→- IF ELSE THEN, BEGIN UNTIL AGAIN WHILE REPEAT
    59→- VARIABLE, CONSTANT
    60→- DO LOOP (optional)
    61→- .S, WORDS (debugging tools)
    62→- Test: Forth-defined words work, fibonacci in Forth
    63→
    64→### Phase 6: Board Integration (future)
    65→- IO@ IO! for memory-mapped I/O
    66→- LED/button words
    67→- Interrupt support (deferred)

```bash
# Step 1: START

# Step 3: WORK

# Step 4: COMMIT

# Step 5: COMPLETE
agentrail complete --summary "what you accomplished" \
  --reward 1 \
  --actions "tools and approach used"

# If failed: --reward -1 --failure-mode "what went wrong"
# If saga finished: add --done flag

# Step 6: STOP
```

- **Crates** (Cargo workspace, edition 2024):
  - `agentrail-core` — Domain types, SagaConfig, StepConfig, Trajectory, HandoffPacket
  - `agentrail-store` — File-based persistence against `.agentrail/` (saga, step, trajectory, session modules)
  - `agentrail-cli` — Binary (8 commands: init, status, next, begin, complete, plan, history, abort)

  ```
  .agentrail/trajectories/{task_type}/run_NNN.json
  .agentrail/domains.toml
  .agentrail/sessions/{session-id}.jsonl
  ```
- **Key Concepts**:

  - **Step Roles**: Meta, Production, Deterministic, Validation
  - **ICRL Injection**: `agentrail next` retrieves successful experiences and injects them into prompt

  - `agentrail abort` — Stop current step

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## CRITICAL: AgentRail Session Protocol (MUST follow exactly)

This project uses AgentRail. Every session follows this exact sequence:

### 1. START (do this FIRST, before anything else)
```bash
agentrail next
```
Read the output carefully. It tells you your current step, prompt, skill docs, and past trajectories.

### 2. BEGIN (immediately after reading the next output)
```bash
agentrail begin
```

### 3. WORK (do what the step prompt says)
Do NOT ask the user "want me to proceed?" or "shall I start?". The step prompt IS your instruction. Execute it.

### 4. COMMIT (after the work is done)
Commit your code changes with git.

### 5. COMPLETE (LAST thing, after committing)
```bash
agentrail complete --summary "what you accomplished" \
  --reward 1 \
  --actions "tools and approach used"
```
If the step failed: `--reward -1 --failure-mode "what went wrong"`
If the saga is finished: add `--done`

### 6. STOP (after complete, DO NOT continue working)
Do NOT make any further code changes after running agentrail complete.
Any changes after complete are untracked and invisible to the next session.
If you see more work to do, it belongs in the NEXT step, not this session.

Do NOT skip any of these steps. The next session depends on your trajectory recording.

## Project: web-tf24a -- Forth Debugger on COR24

Browser-based Forth debugger running the tf24a DTC Forth interpreter on the cor24-rs emulator via WASM.

### Build and Serve

```bash
trunk build                    # Build WASM to dist/
./scripts/serve.sh             # Dev server on port 9181
./scripts/build-pages.sh       # Release build to pages/ for GitHub Pages
cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings  # Lint
cargo fmt --all                # Format
```

### Utilities

- `ep2ms` — returns milliseconds since epoch; use for `?ts=` cache-busting on image URLs in README (e.g. `screenshot.png?ts=$(ep2ms)`)

### Architecture

- **Trunk** builds the WASM binary and serves it
- **cor24-emulator** provides `EmulatorCore` + `Assembler` (path dep to `../../sw-embed/cor24-rs`)
- **Yew 0.21** CSR framework for the UI
- Assembly files in `asm/` are `include_str!`'d and assembled at runtime
- UART I/O bridges user input to the Forth interpreter running in the emulator

### Key Files

- `src/debugger.rs` -- Main debugger component (emulator loop, UI panels)
- `src/config.rs` -- ForthTier enum (multi-tier assembly) + StackSize
- `src/demos.rs` -- Demo registry (embedded .fth files)
- `demos/*.fth` -- Forth demo source files
- `asm/forth-bootstrap.s` -- Phase 1 tf24a Forth kernel (copied from tf24a)
- `asm/forth-interpreter.s` -- Phase 4 full interpreter (copied from tf24a)
- `index.html` -- Entry point with high-contrast dark theme
- `src/debugger.css` -- Debugger panel styling
- `build.rs` -- Build script (BUILD_SHA, BUILD_HOST, BUILD_TIMESTAMP)
- `scripts/build-pages.sh` -- Release build to pages/ for GitHub Pages
- `.github/workflows/pages.yml` -- Deploy pages/ on push to main

### COR24 Register Allocation (from tf24a)

- r0 = W (work/scratch)
- r1 = RSP (return stack pointer, SRAM ~0x0F0000 growing down)
- r2 = IP (instruction pointer for threaded code)
- sp = DSP (data stack, hardware push/pop in EBR)
- fp = available as extra scratch
- Cell size = 3 bytes (24-bit words)

The `pages/` directory is committed and deployed via GitHub Actions (`.github/workflows/pages.yml`).
Do NOT run `trunk build` or `trunk serve` directly — use the scripts.

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## CRITICAL: AgentRail Session Protocol (MUST follow exactly)

This project uses AgentRail. Every session follows this exact sequence:

### 1. START (do this FIRST, before anything else)
```bash
agentrail next
```
Read the output carefully. It tells you your current step, prompt, skill docs, and past trajectories.

### 2. BEGIN (immediately after reading the next output)
```bash
agentrail begin
```

### 3. WORK (do what the step prompt says)
Do NOT ask the user "want me to proceed?" or "shall I start?". The step prompt IS your instruction. Execute it.

### 4. COMMIT (after the work is done)
Commit your code changes with git.

### 5. COMPLETE (LAST thing, after committing)
```bash
agentrail complete --summary "what you accomplished" \
  --reward 1 \
  --actions "tools and approach used"
```
If the step failed: `--reward -1 --failure-mode "what went wrong"`
If the saga is finished: add `--done`

### 6. STOP (after complete, DO NOT continue working)
Do NOT make any further code changes after running agentrail complete.
Any changes after complete are untracked and invisible to the next session.
If you see more work to do, it belongs in the NEXT step, not this session.

Do NOT skip any of these steps. The next session depends on your trajectory recording.

## Project: pv24a — P-Code VM for COR24 ISA

A p-code virtual machine and p-code assembler (pasm), both written in COR24
assembly (.s files). The Pascal compiler is a **separate project** — this
project only implements the VM and the assembler used to test it.

## File Extensions

- `.s` — COR24 assembly source (VM interpreter, pasm assembler)
- `.spc` — P-code assembler source (input to pasm)
- `.p24` — Assembled p-code bytecode (output of pasm, input to VM)

## Key Documentation (READ BEFORE WORKING)

- `docs/research.txt` — Deep research on p-code VM design, memory model, calling conventions, instruction set, and p-code assembler design. Source of truth for design rationale.
- `docs/design.md` — VM specification: opcodes, encoding table, call frame layout, calling convention, pasm syntax, trap codes, memory segments.
- `docs/architecture.md` — Layered system architecture, memory map, register allocation, component map.
- `docs/prd.md` — Product requirements, deliverables, success criteria.

## Related Projects

- `~/github/sw-vibe-coding/tf24a` — DTC Forth in COR24 assembly (pattern reference for .s project structure, testing, CLAUDE.md)
- `~/github/sw-embed/cor24-rs` — COR24 assembler and emulator (Rust, provides `cor24-run`)
- `~/github/sw-vibe-coding/agentrail-domain-coding` — Coding skills domain

## Available Task Types

`cor24-asm`, `pre-commit`

## Build & Test

```bash
# Assemble and run a .s file
cor24-run --run <file.s> --speed 0

# With UART input
cor24-run --run <file.s> -u 'input text\n' --speed 0 -n 5000000

# Dump CPU state after halt
cor24-run --run <file.s> --dump --speed 0

# Trace last N instructions
cor24-run --run <file.s> --trace 100 --speed 0

# Interactive REPL mode
cor24-run --run <file.s> --terminal --echo
```

## COR24 ISA Constraints (MUST follow)

- **3 GPRs only**: r0, r1, r2 (loads/ALU destination)
- **fp**: only usable as base register for indexed memory ops
- **sp**: hardware stack in EBR, cannot receive load results
- **Cell size**: 3 bytes (24-bit words)
- **Labels**: must be on their own line (`label:` not `label: instr`)
- **Comments**: semicolon only (`;`)
- **No string literals**: use `.byte 72, 101, 108, 108, 111` for "Hello"
- **No hex in assembler**: use decimal (`-65280` not `0xFF0100`)
- **Branch offset**: ±127 bytes; far jumps: `la r0, label; jmp (r0)`
- **`.word label`**: emits 24-bit address (one label per directive)

## Register Allocation (for VM interpreter)

```
r0 = W (work/scratch, opcode dispatch)
r1 = VM state pointer or secondary scratch
r2 = scratch
sp = COR24 hardware stack (EBR, use sparingly)
fp = memory base for indexed loads/stores
```

## UART I/O

- Data register: -65280 (0xFF0100)
- Status register: -65279 (0xFF0101)
- TX busy: bit 7 (sign bit when sign-extended via `lb`)
- RX ready: bit 0
- LED port: -65024 (0xFF0200)

## Project: web-dv24r -- P-Code VM Debugger on COR24

Browser-based p-code VM debugger running the pv24a VM on the cor24-rs emulator via WASM. Two-level debugging: p-code semantic level (primary) and COR24 host implementation level (secondary drill-down).

## Related Projects

- `~/github/softwarewrighter/pv24a` -- P-code VM and p-code assembler (COR24 assembly)
- `~/github/sw-vibe-coding/pv24a` -- P-code VM (alternate location)
- `~/github/sw-vibe-coding/tc24r` -- COR24 C compiler (Rust)
- `~/github/sw-embed/cor24-rs` -- COR24 assembler and emulator (Rust)
- `~/github/sw-vibe-coding/web-tf24a` -- Forth debugger (pattern reference for debugger UI)
- `~/github/sw-vibe-coding/web-tml24c` -- Lisp REPL (pattern reference for Yew UI)
- `~/github/sw-vibe-coding/web-tc24r` -- C compiler UI (pattern reference)
- `~/github/sw-vibe-coding/agentrail-domain-coding` -- Coding skills domain

## Available Task Types

`rust-project-init`, `rust-clippy-fix`, `yew-component`, `wasm-build`, `pre-commit`

## Key Documentation (READ BEFORE WORKING)

- `docs/research.txt` -- Deep research on p-code VM design, memory model, calling conventions, instruction set, p-code assembler design, and Pascal compiler architecture.
- `docs/debugger.txt` -- Debugger UI design: panels, state model, stepping semantics, what to show/hide, extensibility for watches and breakpoints.

## Build

Edition 2024 for any Rust code. Never suppress warnings.

```bash
trunk build                    # Build WASM to dist/
./scripts/serve.sh             # Dev server
./scripts/build-pages.sh       # Release build to pages/ for GitHub Pages
cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings  # Lint
cargo fmt --all                # Format
```

## Utilities

- `ep2ms` -- returns milliseconds since epoch; use for `?ts=` cache-busting on image URLs in README

## Architecture

- **Trunk** builds the WASM binary and serves it
- **cor24-emulator** provides `EmulatorCore` + `Assembler` (path dep to `../../sw-embed/cor24-rs`)
- **Yew 0.21** CSR framework for the UI
- Component-based architecture using `Component` trait with `Msg` enum for state updates
- Assembly files in `asm/` are `include_str!`'d and assembled at runtime
- Batch execution loop (50K instructions per tick) prevents browser blocking
- UART I/O bridges user input to the VM running in the emulator

## Key Files (to be created)

- `src/debugger.rs` -- Main debugger component (emulator loop, UI panels)
- `src/config.rs` -- VM configuration (tier/mode selection)
- `src/demos.rs` -- Demo registry (embedded .spc/.pasm files)
- `asm/` -- VM assembly files (include_str!'d from pv24a)
- `index.html` -- Entry point with Catppuccin Mocha theme
- `src/debugger.css` -- Debugger panel styling
- `build.rs` -- Build script (BUILD_SHA, BUILD_HOST, BUILD_TIMESTAMP)
- `scripts/serve.sh` -- Dev server script
- `scripts/build-pages.sh` -- Release build to pages/
- `.github/workflows/pages.yml` -- Deploy pages/ on push to main

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## CRITICAL: AgentRail Session Protocol (MUST follow exactly)

This project uses AgentRail. Every session follows this exact sequence:

## Project: web-dv24r -- P-Code VM Debugger on COR24

Browser-based p-code VM debugger running the pv24a VM on the cor24-rs emulator via WASM. Two-level debugging: p-code semantic level (primary) and COR24 host implementation level (secondary drill-down).

## Related Projects

- `~/github/softwarewrighter/pv24a` -- P-code VM and p-code assembler (COR24 assembly)
- `~/github/sw-vibe-coding/pv24a` -- P-code VM (alternate location)
- `~/github/sw-vibe-coding/tc24r` -- COR24 C compiler (Rust)
- `~/github/sw-embed/cor24-rs` -- COR24 assembler and emulator (Rust)
- `~/github/sw-vibe-coding/web-tf24a` -- Forth debugger (pattern reference for debugger UI)
- `~/github/sw-vibe-coding/web-tml24c` -- Lisp REPL (pattern reference for Yew UI)
- `~/github/sw-vibe-coding/web-tc24r` -- C compiler UI (pattern reference)
- `~/github/sw-vibe-coding/agentrail-domain-coding` -- Coding skills domain

## Available Task Types

`rust-project-init`, `rust-clippy-fix`, `yew-component`, `wasm-build`, `pre-commit`

## Key Documentation (READ BEFORE WORKING)

- `docs/research.txt` -- Deep research on p-code VM design, memory model, calling conventions, instruction set, p-code assembler design, and Pascal compiler architecture.
- `docs/debugger.txt` -- Debugger UI design: panels, state model, stepping semantics, what to show/hide, extensibility for watches and breakpoints.

## Build

Edition 2024 for any Rust code. Never suppress warnings.

```bash
trunk build                    # Build WASM to dist/
./scripts/serve.sh             # Dev server
./scripts/build-pages.sh       # Release build to pages/ for GitHub Pages
cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings  # Lint
cargo fmt --all                # Format
```

## Utilities

- `ep2ms` -- returns milliseconds since epoch; use for `?ts=` cache-busting on image URLs in README

## Architecture

- **Trunk** builds the WASM binary and serves it
- **cor24-emulator** provides `EmulatorCore` + `Assembler` (path dep to `../../sw-embed/cor24-rs`)
- **Yew 0.21** CSR framework for the UI
- Component-based architecture using `Component` trait with `Msg` enum for state updates
- Assembly files in `asm/` are `include_str!`'d and assembled at runtime
- Batch execution loop (50K instructions per tick) prevents browser blocking
- UART I/O bridges user input to the VM running in the emulator

## Key Files (to be created)

- `src/debugger.rs` -- Main debugger component (emulator loop, UI panels)
- `src/config.rs` -- VM configuration (tier/mode selection)
- `src/demos.rs` -- Demo registry (embedded .spc/.pasm files)
- `asm/` -- VM assembly files (include_str!'d from pv24a)
- `index.html` -- Entry point with Catppuccin Mocha theme
- `src/debugger.css` -- Debugger panel styling
- `build.rs` -- Build script (BUILD_SHA, BUILD_HOST, BUILD_TIMESTAMP)
- `scripts/serve.sh` -- Dev server script
- `scripts/build-pages.sh` -- Release build to pages/
- `.github/workflows/pages.yml` -- Deploy pages/ on push to main

message.content: good enough to use with a UI as features are added, right?

content.text:
Yes -- the VM has stack ops, arithmetic, control flow, frames, memory access, non-local variable access, and syscalls already implemented. That's enough to run p-code programs and build a debugger UI against. Traps (step 9) and the pasm assembler (steps 10-14) are still in progress, but you can start the UI now and wire up new VM features as they land.

The existing web debuggers (web-tf24a, web-tml24c) followed the same pattern -- the UI was built while the underlying implementation was still evolving.
